migration_plan.title: SGA Development to Production Migration Plan
migration_plan.description: Migration of the GHS Label System and connected services to the server that currently hosts the Database/SMB Share. Moving from network-based file sharing (SMB) to direct local file access architecture.

tasks.description: Ensure your requirements.txt is fully up-to-date. If not, generate a fresh one using: pip freeze > requirements.txt
Backup your local unified_db, history.json, users.json, and the generated_labels directory.
The application depends on Poppler (for PDF generation / ReportLab). Prepare the Poppler binaries (like poppler-24.08.0) for transfer.

Install the exact same version of Python (ensure 'Add Python to PATH' is checked). Install Git if you plan to clone the repository, or prepare a directory to receive the files (e.g., C:\SGA_Production).
Copy the poppler-24.08.0 folder to C:\poppler-24.08.0 on the server. Add C:\poppler-24.08.0\Library\bin to the server's System Environment Variables (PATH).
If the GUI client or Web interface will be accessed by other machines from the server, ensure inbound ports are open (e.g., Web Server on TCP 5000, 8080).
Ensure the SAP HANA ODBC driver (hdbcli) and related database dependencies are installed on the server so sap_connector.py can bridge to SAP.
Copy the SGAv1.01 directory to the server (e.g., C:\SGA_Production\SGAv1.01). DO NOT copy the .venv folder. Python virtual environments are strictly tied to the system path they were created on.

Open a PowerShell terminal as Administrator on the server inside the new directory: python -m venv .venv -> .\.venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1 -> python -m pip install --upgrade pip -> pip install -r requirements.txt

phases.name: Configuration Restructuring
phases.description: Crucial step. Since the code is now living on the machine that hosts the Database, db_client_config.json and shared_config.json must change to reflect that it is no longer a 'client' connecting over the network.

tasks.description: Change 'deployment_type' from 'local' to 'server'. Change 'shared_base_path' from '\\192.168.2.237\SGA_Database' to the actual local volume path where those files reside on the server (e.g., 'C:\SGA_Database'). Update the 'network' block to disable network mounting: mount_check_enabled = false, fallback_to_local = true, server_address = 127.0.0.1.
Change 'deployment_type' to 'server'. The 'primary_path' should point to the local disk path (e.g., 'C:\SGA_Database'). Overwrite 'web_server_host' in watchdog to '127.0.0.1' or '0.0.0.0'.
Review sap_connector.py or .env vars (if any) to ensure the SAP DB credentials work from the IP of the server.
Run 'python test_database_system.py' to ensure local unified DB loading works.
Run 'python test_sap_connection.py' to confirm the HANA ODBC works from the new server.
Run 'python sga_web/app.py' and open a browser to 'http://127.0.0.1:5000'.
Run 'python ghs_label_gui.py', load a mock label, and print it. This will validate that Poppler is properly in the system PATH.

Configure watchdog.py and database_server.py as Windows Services (using NSSM) or via Windows Task Scheduler (Trigger: 'On System Startup', Action: Start a program, Path: '.venv\Scripts\pythonw.exe', Arguments: 'watchdog.py').
Ensure unified_db and config JSON backup routines are running properly via Windows Task Scheduler.
